title: Exception Handling
simple_definition: Exception handling lets your program deal with errors gracefully instead of crashing.
technical_definition: Python uses try/except/else/finally blocks. Exceptions are objects inheriting from BaseException.
analogy: Exception handling is like a safety net — catch errors before they crash your program.
output: # Run the code to see output
logic_steps: Understand the core concept of Exception Handling
Trace through the example step by step
edge_cases: Always test with edge cases: empty input, None, zero
Check for type errors when mixing data types
Consider what happens with very large or very small values
common_mistakes: Skipping the basics and jumping to complex code
Not testing code with different inputs
interview_tips: Exception Handling is commonly tested in Python interviews
Understand the time and space complexity
Be ready to explain your reasoning

problem_first:
Your program asks user to enter a number.
User types 'hello' instead.

Without exception handling:
num = int(input())  # ValueError: invalid literal!
# Program CRASHES! 💥

With exception handling:
try:
    num = int(input())
except ValueError:
    print('Please enter a valid number')
# Program continues gracefully ✅
why_matters:
Exception handling is what separates amateur code from production code:
🌐 Web apps: handle bad requests without crashing
📁 File ops: handle missing files gracefully
�� APIs: handle network errors
🗄️ Databases: handle connection failures

A program that crashes on bad input is not production-ready.
important_points: ⭐ try block: code that might raise an exception
⭐ except block: runs if exception occurs — catches and handles it
⭐ else block: runs ONLY if NO exception occurred
⭐ finally block: ALWAYS runs — use for cleanup (close files, connections)
⭐ raise keyword: manually raise an exception
⭐ Custom exceptions: inherit from Exception class
⭐ except Exception as e: catches ALL exceptions — use sparingly
⭐ Never use bare except: — always specify the exception type

steps.action: Exception caught! Jump to except block
